title: Gastrosiphon vespertilionis
intro: Gastrosiphon vespertilionis, also known as the bat orchid or evening orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Traditionally, Gastrosiphon vespertilionis is used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Gastrosiphon vespertilionis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Gastrosiphon vespertilionis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make topical creams or salves to treat skin conditions.
